title: devmate config
$comment: Shared contract between devmate (consumer / eyes) and monoroot (producer / hands). The extension is the sole writer of the merged multi-root config; devmate reads and validates. Versioning policy: additive fields do NOT bump schemaVersion; only a breaking restructure does. `source`/`synthesized`/`contractVersion` are additive and stay at schemaVersion 2. Runtime validation on both sides is hand-written (devmate has zero runtime deps); the executable agreement between the two validators is the shared fixtures corpus in test/fixtures/config-contract, pinned by contractVersion. Keep this file byte-identical between the two repos when the contract changes.

schemaVersion.description: 1 = single-repo config authored by `devmate init`; 2 = merged multi-root config written by the extension.

contractVersion.description: Producer-stamped version of the shared config contract (this schema + the fixtures corpus) the merged config was written against. Additive — never bumps schemaVersion. The consumer compares it against its own pinned contract version and emits a non-blocking skew nudge on mismatch.

mode.description: Structural discriminator. Absent = single-root. Never coupled to schemaVersion.

primary.description: Multi-root only — name of the primary repo (must appear in repos).

repos.description: Multi-root only — repo subdirectory names that make up the workspace.

personas.description: Edit-boundary owners. Never empty — the producer synthesizes a fallback persona for any un-init'd repo.

repo.description: Multi-root only — the sub-repo subdirectory this persona targets. Globs are anchored against it.

source.description: Producer provenance stamp. 'repo' = authored in a sub-repo's config; 'fallback' = synthesized for an un-init'd repo.

synthesized.description: True when the producer invented this persona because the repo had no usable config. Pairs with source === 'fallback'.

domains.description: DN-1: optional business-domain ownership map — maps a task's business vocabulary to the file clusters and invariants of that domain. Absent = today's behavior (no-op).

globs.description: Repo-relative globs owning this domain's files.

contextFile.description: Repo-relative path to the domain context markdown, or null.

entryPoints.description: Repo-relative FILE paths that anchor the domain (not symbols).

sessionArtifactPaths.description: Globs of session artifacts no agent may hand-edit — gate state, the human-approved spec, the evidence chain. Absent = the protective default ['.devmate/state/**', '.devmate/session/**']: omitting this key PROTECTS, it never disables. Every protected artifact is written by a devmate hook, and a hook is not a tool call, so the deny costs the workflow nothing.

sessionArtifactWriters.description: Identity-gated exceptions to sessionArtifactPaths — the agents permitted to write the artifacts a glob matches. Absent = the protective default [{ glob: '.devmate/session/**/spec.md', agents: ['spec-writer'] }]: spec.md is the one protected artifact an agent, not a hook, produces. Fails closed on a malformed entry — a half-read writer roster would widen the boundary it exists to narrow.

glob.description: Glob selecting the protected artifacts this exception covers.

agents.description: Agent names allowed to write those artifacts (e.g. 'spec-writer').

acCoverageGate.description: AC-2 (epic #416): gates verification-passed/pr-ready on AC-1's coverage read. 'off' (default) is a no-op; 'warn' records a contract_violation trace event but allows the transition; 'block' refuses it.

checks.description: Canonical variable-length verification list, fit to the codebase. Mirrors the consumer's hand validator: each check needs a unique non-empty id, a command, and an OPEN category string (never an enum). The legacy unitTest/typeCheck/e2e keys below are deprecated input the consumer normalizes into checks on load.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier, unique within checks.

command.description: Opaque command text — rendered into dispatch payloads, never auto-executed.

category.description: Open label (conventional: unit-test, type-check, e2e, lint, format, build, audit, contract, integration, ...). The category 'unit-test' check drives the TDD gate.

source.description: Grounding pointer to where the command was found (e.g. package.json#scripts.test); '[UNVERIFIED]' marks an ungrounded proposal.
